title: Fixture directory split into types/jmap/session/methods/ layers is worth preserving
description: Fixtures are separated into types/ (single data-type objects), jmap/ (core wire envelopes), session/ (Session validation cases), and methods/ (full JmapResponse bodies per method family). A contributor might flatten everything into a single directory. The layered structure makes it immediately clear what a fixture tests: a methods/ fixture exercises the full dispatch path; a types/ fixture tests only deserialization of a leaf object. This distinction matters when diagnosing a test failure: a types/ failure is a serde issue; a methods/ failure could also be extract_response or build_request.
